name: Carisma Therapeutics Inc.
summary: Carisma Therapeutics Inc. is a biotechnology company focused on macrophage and monocyte engineering to develop therapies for liver fibrosis and cancer. Its proprietary CAR-M platform uses chimeric antigen receptors to redirect macrophages against tumor-associated antigens. Key candidates include CT-2401 (liver fibrosis, mRNA/LNP approach) and CT-1119 (mesothelin-positive solid tumors). Following strategic reviews in 2024-2025, the company paused R&D and is exploring strategic alternatives including asset sales, partnerships, or a company merger.

products.name: CT-2401
products.description: mRNA/LNP-based product candidate targeting liver fibrosis via engineered macrophages; achieved pre-clinical proof of concept in Q2 2024.

products.name: CT-1119
products.description: Mesothelin-targeted CAR-Monocyte product candidate for advanced solid tumors including pancreatic, ovarian, and lung cancer; development paused.

products.name: CT-0525
products.description: HER2-directed CAR-Monocyte autologous cell therapy for solid tumors; development ceased in December 2024 after Phase 1 trial.

products.name: CT-0508
products.description: Macrophage-based initial lead product candidate; all clinical activities ceased as of the 2024 revised operating plan.

products.name: CAR-M Platform
products.description: Proprietary chimeric antigen receptor macrophage platform for redirecting macrophages and monocytes against tumor-associated antigens.

partnerships.description: Collaboration on in vivo mRNA/LNP CAR-M programs; Carisma eligible to receive future milestones and royalty payments.
